summary: Gruber announces an expanded set of prizes for the Daring Fireball membership drive, listing software donations from numerous Mac developers including Ranchero, Bare Bones, The Iconfactory, Rogue Amoeba, and others. The total retail value exceeds $1,600. He extends the membership drive through July 2 and explains the random selection process using a Perl script. Members can optionally rank their prize preferences via email.
key_points: The membership drive prizes grew from a single NetNewsWire donation to over $1,600 worth of Mac software from numerous indie developers
The prize list reads as a who's-who of mid-2000s Mac indie software: BBEdit, NetNewsWire, Path Finder, Audio Hijack Pro, VoodooPad, and more
The contest uses a simple, transparent random selection via Perl script, with an optional preference-ranking system
The breadth of donations reflects the tight-knit Mac developer community rallying around Gruber's independent writing
money_quotes: The retail value of all the above software is a little over $1,300 $1,600 (!).
Winners will be selected randomly, using a simple Perl script.
Each member can only win once. I.e. if there are 21 prizes, there will be 21 winners.
key_insight: The generous response from Mac indie developers to Gruber's membership drive illustrates the strong community bonds that characterized the mid-2000s Mac software ecosystem.
